title: Diagram history dropdown component with version preview
purpose: Provides a dropdown interface to view and select historical versions of a diagram, showing relative timestamps and loading states.
module_context: Exports a React functional component DiagramHistoryDropdown that accepts diagram ID, version selection callback, and optional CSS classes. Uses a custom hook to fetch diagram history and manages dropdown open/close state with click-outside detection.

steps.content: Parse the input timestamp into a date object.
Compare the date to today's date to determine if it's today.
IF the date is today, format and return just the time portion.
ELSE check if the date is yesterday and format accordingly.
ELSE calculate the difference in days from today and check if it's within the last 7 days.
IF within 7 days, format with day name and time.
ELSE format with month, day, and time for older dates.
Initialize state for dropdown open status and loading timestamp.
Create a ref for the dropdown element to detect outside clicks.
Fetch diagram history and version loading function using a custom hook.
Determine if history exists and calculate version count.
Set up click outside detection to close the dropdown when clicking elsewhere.
Handle item click by loading version content and calling selection callback.
Render the dropdown button with appropriate styling and disabled states.
IF dropdown is open and history exists, render the version list with loading indicators.
Set the loading timestamp to indicate which version is being loaded.
Fetch the content for the specified timestamp using the version loading function.
Clear the loading timestamp after content is retrieved.
IF content is successfully retrieved, call the version selection callback with timestamp and content.
Close the dropdown after successful version selection.
Check if the clicked target is outside the dropdown element.
IF the click is outside, close the dropdown by setting open state to false.
